category_title: At-risk four-year-old students exhibiting varied oral language behaviors
student_description: This is an at-risk four-year-old student with delayed language development who relies on one-word responses with adults due to a fear of making grammatical errors, despite using more complex language with peers. They occasionally resort to aggressive language during conflicts. To build their expressive language confidence, they uniquely need adults to avoid direct corrections and compliance-based commands, instead requiring open-ended questions, recasts of their errors, and elaborations on their short statements.

criteria.rationale: Consistently hearing full, grammatically correct sentences over time provides the strong language model this student needs to overcome their delays.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term communication strategy relies exclusively on complete, grammatically correct sentences, intentionally avoiding fragmented or one-word replies to the student.

criteria.rationale: Dramatic play and one-on-one experiences naturally foster the extended, non-directive conversations required to build the student's expressive language confidence.
criteria.check: The tutor's long-term plan incorporates dramatic play or dedicated one-on-one time designed specifically to elicit extended, multi-turn conversations rather than just academic quizzing.
